South Korea's highest-paid math tutor makes $14 million a year, more than Son Heung-min, their most famous footballer. His name is Hyun Woo-jin. He graduated from Stanford's math department in three years and three months. His textbook series, Neuron, sells over a million copies a year. Every November, South Korea pauses for a single nine-hour exam called the Suneung. The Air Force halts training flights. For 35 minutes during the English listening section, no commercial flights can take off or land at Incheon, one of Asia's busiest airports. About 8,000 police officers escort late students on motorcycles with sirens running. Inside that exam, the most consequential questions are called killer questions. They're deliberately designed to fall outside the public school curriculum. They exist to separate the top one percent from everyone else, because that one percent gets into Seoul National, Korea, and Yonsei. The SKY universities that produce around 30% of Korea's top-1,000-company CEOs. Parents can't evaluate the curriculum themselves. They can't reverse-engineer the killer question. So they borrow Hyun Woo-jin's judgment. If your child went through Neuron and still failed, you did what every reasonable parent in the neighborhood does. The fault is your child's, or fate's. Not yours. That's what's actually being sold. Not math. Certainty. Korean households spent 29.2 trillion won on private tutoring in 2024, an all-time record, even as the school-age population dropped another 1.5%. A country is, in a measurable sense, choosing not to exist in order to keep paying for that certainty.

so we can officially make the viral dot cake at home and it’s my new favorite little treat 🧁🌈 Ingredients: 1 box @Funfetti cake mix 3 eggs 1 cup water 1/2 cup oil funfetti vanilla frosting rainbow nonpareils sprinkles Instructions: In a large mixing bowl, mix cake mix, eggs, water, and oil until well combined and transfer to a greased square baking pan. Bake at 350° for 35-40 minutes, covering with foil halfway through. Allow to cool completely, then press greased ramekin into the center and twist to remove. Allow mini cake to slide out of ramekin and slice through the center to create 2 layers. Place bottom layer back in ramekin, top with a layer of frosting, then place top layer of cake with another layer of frosting spread evenly to the edges. Cover in rainbow nonpareils, allow to chill and slightly harden in the fridge, then take a center bite and enjoy! 😋 #cake #Recipe #baking #dessert #creatorsearchinsights

What Your Cat Thinks When You Leave Every time you walk out the door, your cat has no idea where you're going. They don't know what work or school is. To them, you're out hunting and they're just hoping you make it back safe. And the longer you're gone, the more they feel it. Cats are way more attached to you than most people think. They just show it in tiny quiet ways. If you have a cat, make sure someone checks on them when you're away for more than a day. They need more than just food and water. #cats #catbehavior #catstiktok #catfacts #PetsOfTikTok

STOP BUYING store-bought cookies… make these instead.✨ The best part? If you break down the exact amount of ingredients used, these cookies cost less than $10 to make. Here’s your next pot luck dish or hostess gift👍 I love making these dairy free so they can easily be served after a meat meal, but you can absolutely use regular butter and dairy fillings if you prefer. Ingredients: • 3½ cups all purpose flour (500 g) • ¾ tablespoon baking powder (10 g) • ½ cup powdered sugar (75 g), plus more for dusting • 3 sticks non-dairy butter (340 g)  • ⅓ cup oil (80 mL) • Pinch of salt • Slightly less than ¼ cup water (about 50–55 mL) Water can be substituted with orange juice or milk if making dairy cookies. Fillings: • Plant-based Nutella • Crushed hazelnuts • Date spread • Crumbled halva • Biscoff cookie butter • Crushed Biscoff cookies Instructions: Place all dough ingredients into a mixer or large bowl and combine until a soft, pliable dough forms. If the dough feels too dry, add a little more liquid, 1 tablespoon at a time. If it feels too wet, add a little more flour. The dough is very forgiving. Divide dough into 3 equal pieces. Place dough between 2 sheets of parchment paper and roll into a thin rectangle or oval, about ⅛ inch thick. Spread your filling of choice and add toppings. Using the parchment paper to help guide you, carefully roll the dough into a log, gently peeling back the parchment as you go. Using a knife or bench scraper, cut cookies about ¾ inch thick and transfer onto a parchment-lined baking sheet. I like using a bench scraper to help lift the cookies. Repeat with remaining dough. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for about 25–30 minutes. Once completely cooled, dust generously with powdered sugar and enjoy. These freeze beautifully. Store in a cookie jar on the counter, in the refrigerator for extra freshness, or freeze the dough ahead of time until needed. #sivanskitchen #easy #baking #cookies #savemoney

Strawberry Nutella Brownies 🍫💌🍓 So fudgy & perfect for valentines!  (Recipe makes an 8x8 pan) Ingredients: 10 tbsp (140g) unsalted butter, melted 1 cup (200g) granulated sugar ¼ cup (50g) brown sugar, packed  2 eggs, room temp 1 tsp vanilla extract ½ cup + 2 tbsp (56g) unsweetened cocoa powder ½ tsp salt 1 tbsp (13g) vegetable oil ½ cup (63g) all purpose flour, spooned & leveled or weighed (don’t pack it in) ¾ cup chocolate chips/ chunks 190g (~⅔ cup) Nutella  Topping: strawberry slices, patted dry (as many as you want/enough to cover the top) Nutella (to spread over the top and drizzle)  *if the nutella is too thick to drizzle you can melt it slightly in the microwave Instructions: Line an 8x8 pan with parchment paper. Spread the nutella onto the parchment into a thin, even square the size of the pan. Freeze for at least 20-30 minutes. Preheat oven to 325°F (163°C) and line/grease an 8x8-inch pan with parchment. In a large bowl, whisk melted butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar until smooth. Add eggs and vanilla, then whisk vigorously for 1-2 minutes to help create a crackly top. Stir in cocoa powder and salt until fully combined. Mix in oil, then gently fold in the flour until just combined (don’t over mix). Fold in chocolate chips/chopped chocolate. Spread half the batter into the greased pan. Place the frozen nutella sheet on top of it. Then, spread the remaining batter over it to cover the nutella layer completely. Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until a toothpick near the center comes out with no wet batter. It should just come out with a little nutella & slightly moist crumbs. Let the brownies cool completely. Spread a thin layer of nutella over the cooled brownies, add the strawberry slices (patted dry) all over the top, and drizzle with more nutella.  Best enjoyed fresh!   #bakingrecipe #fudgybrownies #chocolatestrawberries #nutella #Recipe

So fluffy & delicious!🤎 Recipe below👇🏼  🍩 Ingredients Dough: * 300g strong white bread flour * 30g caster sugar * 1/2 tsp salt * 7g fast-action yeast * 2 eggs * 75ml warm milk * 50g melted butter Chocolate Pastry Cream: * 540g whole milk * 2 tsp vanilla extract * 18g cocoa powder * 2 eggs * 2 egg yolks * 100g brown sugar * 25g cornstarch * ¼ tsp sea salt * 170g milk chocolate, chopped * 30g butter 🧾Instructions 1. Make the Dough Mix flour, sugar, salt, and yeast in a bowl. Add eggs, warm milk, and melted butter. Knead until elastic by hand or in mixer. Cover and let rise for 1 hour. 2. Shape & Proof Punch down the dough and divide into 65g portions. Roll each piece into a smooth ball and place onto individual squares of baking paper. Arrange on a tray, cover loosely, and let rise for another hour.  3. Make Chocolate Cream Heat milk and vanilla until steaming. In a bowl, whisk cocoa, eggs, yolks, sugar, cornstarch, and salt. Slowly add the hot milk while whisking constantly, then return to pan and cook until thick & bubbling. Off heat, stir in chocolate and butter. Chill with plastic wrap touching the surface. 4. Fry the Donuts Fry donuts in hot oil. Fry until golden. Drain and roll in sugar. 5. Fill Transfer the chilled chocolate pastry cream to a piping bag fitted with a round nozzle. Poke a small hole in the side of each donut and pipe in the filling generously. Ooey-Gooey Scoopable Nutella Cookie Pie🤤 Yields: 15-16 Chunky Cookies INGREDIENTS:  230g Cold Chopped Unsalted Butter  160g Caster Sugar  160g Light Brown Sugar  450g Chocolate Chunks or chips  475g Plain Flour  1 tsp Salt 3 tsp Baking Powder  3/4 tsp Baking Soda  2 Eggs  1 Egg Yolk  1 tsp Vanilla  INSTRUCTIONS: •Cut cling wrap into small squares. Scoop ½ teaspoon Nutella onto individual cling-wrap squares. Fold to seal and freeze until solid. (Repeat for however many Nutella stuffed cookies you plan to make.) •In a large mixing bowl, add the cold cubed butter and whisk until slightly creamy. Add the white sugar and light brown sugar, then whisk together until well combined and fluffy. •Add the eggs, vanilla extract, and chocolate chips or chunks. Whisk until fully incorporated. •Add the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Gently mix until just combined, do not overmix. •Portion the dough into 90g balls. Press a frozen Nutella piece into the centre of each dough ball, seal well, and roll into a smooth ball. •Place the stuffed cookie dough balls onto a tray and freeze for at least 30 minutes (or overnight for best results). •Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). •Take any shape baking dish circle, square, rectangle anything and line it with parchment paper. Arrange frozen cookie dough balls in it leaving some space between them. •Bake for 10-15 minutes (timing depends on the type of pan) or until the tops are lightly golden while the centres remain soft and gooey. •Remove from the oven and let cool slightly. •Serve warm with ice cream and chocolate sauce enjoy!✨ NOTE: Store any extra dough balls in an airtight container or freezer bag and freeze for later. Whenever a cookie craving hits, bake straight from frozen🫶🏻 #cookie #nutella #cookiepie #fyp #viral

Nutella Brownies ✨🧸🍫 Brownie Layer: 110g butter (½ cup) 170g chocolate (1 cup) 200g sugar (1 cup) 2 eggs 80g flour (⅔ cup) 20g cocoa powder (3 tbsp) 1 tsp vanilla 45g chocolate chunks (¼ cup) ½ tsp salt Nutella (about 4–5 tbsp) Extra chocolate chunks Instructions: Melt the butter and chocolate together until smooth. Mix in the sugar, then add the eggs and vanilla. Fold in the flour, cocoa powder, and salt until combined, then mix in the chocolate chunks. Pour half of the brownie batter into a lined baking pan and spread evenly. Add spoonfuls of Nutella in the middle, then cover with the remaining brownie batter. Top with extra chocolate chunks and bake at 175°C until the top is crackly and the center stays fudgy. Enjoy✨ #brownie #baking #chocolate #nutella #recipes

"기계는 재료를 뭉개버린다"는 철학 아래, 매일 새벽부터 나와 모든 재료를 일일이 손으로 버무리고 직접 빚으시더라구요. 재료 하나하나의 식감이 살아있는 꽉 찬 만두 속은 그렇게 사장님의 고집으로 완성됩니다.  워낙 정성껏 소량만 준비하기 때문에 금세 품절되기로 유명한데요. 방문 전 전화 주문이나 네이버 예약을 이용하시면 헛걸음 없이 바로 가져가실 수 있으니 꼭 참고하세요!   주문 즉시 튀겨주시는 치즈 군만두는 갓 튀겨낸 바삭한 식감 속에 고소하게 늘어나는 치즈와 만두 속의 조화가 그야말로 환상적입니다.. 정성과 맛, 어느 하나 빠지지 않는 망원동의 진정한 인생 만두를 만나보세요!   - Deep flavor hand-mixed every dawn: Finding 'Grandmother's Touch' in a Young Master 📍망원 연만두 'Yeon-Mandu' (77, Mangwon-ro, Mapo-gu, Seoul, Republic of Korea)   🔗 https://maps.app.goo.gl/jDhS1Q11mVTn1mBA9   'Yeon-Mandu' is a renowned dumpling spot near Mangwon Market, famous for a deep, traditional flavor that leads many to assume an elderly grandmother must be running the shop. Surprisingly, the true master behind these dumplings is a young man who has dedicated his life to this craft.   The secret to such profound flavor lies in his sincere devotion. Following his philosophy that "machines crush the ingredients' texture," he starts his day in the early hours of the dawn, hand-mixing and folding every single dumpling. This stubborn commitment is what creates the perfect filling, where the texture of each ingredient remains intact and vibrant.   Because each dumpling is made with such meticulous care in limited quantities, they are famous for selling out quickly. We highly recommend placing a phone order or using Naver Reservation before your visit to ensure you don't miss out!  Don't miss the Cheese Fried Dumplings, fried to order for a perfectly crispy texture. The harmony of the savory, melting cheese and the rich dumpling filling is simply heavenly. #mascara                                    Un mascara est un produit de maquillage destiné à être appliqué sur les cils afin de les allonger, les épaissir, les recourber et intensifier le regard. Il se présente généralement sous forme liquide ou crémeuse, contenu dans un tube avec une brosse applicatrice. Le mascara permet de donner plus de volume et de définition aux cils en les colorant, le plus souvent en noir, mais aussi en brun ou en couleurs plus originales. Selon sa formule et la forme de la brosse, il peut offrir différents effets : naturel, volumineux, allongeant ou recourbant. Печенье макаем в молоко и выкладываем в форму (у меня форма 27х18х6), сверху слой крема и делаем таких 3 слоя. Шоколад растапливаем импульсно в микроволновке, добавляем растительное масло (остужаем до комнатной температуры и покрываем торт сверху.
